judge.rationale: Design correctly identifies the 3 domains (projects, tasks, notifications) and file structure matching the reference. Documentation explicitly states ownership and consumer relationships, excellent for onboarding. However, ProjectLookupPort violates minimalism—it includes `getById()` when the reference intentionally exposes only `exists()`, exposing unnecessary implementation details to the consumer (tasks). TaskStoragePort similarly uses `listByProject(projectId)` instead of the reference's more compositional `list(filter?: {projectId})`, making the interface less flexible and harder to extend.
